key_insight: The paper treats token unmasking order in diffusion language models as a learnable “order of thought” policy, and shows that optimizing this schedule can substantially improve generation quality.
engineering_relevance: For practical LLM systems, scheduling is a lightweight control knob that can boost reasoning and decoding performance without changing the frozen denoiser.
method: Derive a tractable upper bound on sequential decoding mismatch using KL divergence and pathwise log-likelihood.
Convert order selection into a policy optimization problem with a dense self-aware reward over ordered trajectories.
Train a lightweight scheduler with Group Relative Policy Optimization and use it for any-order and semi-autoregressive decoding.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong gains on Sudoku and math benchmarks, including 82.0% to 91.8% puzzle accuracy on 1B MDM and GSM8K pass@1 rising from 64% to 76% on LLaDA-8B.
watch_item: Performance depends on learned scheduling and may require additional fine-tuning, so benefits could vary with model size, decoding setup, and training stability.
paper_title: Scheduling Thoughts: Learning the Order of Thought in Diffusion Language Models
